chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD 1st Appeal No.D-09 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 22.04.2026 Mr. Muhammad Yousif Kalhoro, advocate for appellant *** Abdul Wahid S/o Muhammad Yousuf, the appellant-customer is aggrieved by the IInd Banking Court Hyderabad’s judgment and final decree dated 20.01.2026 passed in banking Suit No.481 of 2023. The principle challenge articulated before us today by the appellant’s counsel is that as a result of the coronavirus (Covid-19) pandemic, he fell ill, became sick and suffered huge business losses. He also does not have resources to fulfil the financial obligations on this score too. Additionally because of heavy rains in subsequent years he suffered further losses in his agriculture business and could not meet the payment obligations. Counsel contends that today the appellant is poorer and his brother Zulfiqar Ali also faced similar financial stress and personal challenge to his health. He submits that appellant is ready and willing to pay the entire decreetal amount but seeks repayment by way of either instalments or by way of deferment and/or seeks mercy of this bench on account of the circumstances mentioned above. Heard counsel. With great respect, the submissions pleaded cannot be considered as valid grounds for admission of this first appeal or for that matter to even issue notice to respondent bank – Zarai Taraqiati Bank Limited (“ZTBL”). The appellant’s plea may be agitated before the Executing Court where the matter may be considered on its own merit, subject to all legal consequences. In view of the above, this first appeal is dismissed in limine.

The appellant’s plea may be agitated before the Executing Court where the matter may be considered on its own merit, subject to all legal consequences. In view of the above, this first appeal is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali
